leadText: Growl (Japanese: なきごえ Cry) is a non-damaging Normal-type move introduced in Generation I.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Contents 1 Effect 1.1 Generations I-II 1.2 Generations III-V 1.3 Generation VI onwards 1.4 Generation IX 1.4.1 Pokémon Legends: Z-A Effect [ edit | edit source ] Growl decreases the Attack stat of all adjacent opponents by one stage. Growl is a sound-based move . Pokémon are immune to having their Attack decreased if they have any of the following Abilities: Hyper Cutter , Clear Body , White Smoke , or Full Metal Body . Additionally, Pokémon with Soundproof are immune to Growl due to it being a sound-based move. If powered up by a Normalium Z into Z-Growl, the user's Defense stat rises by one stage, and the user can use this move regardless of whether it was affected by Throat Chop or not. Growl can be used as the second move of a Pokémon Contest combination , with the user gaining 2 bonus appeal points if Charm was used in the previous turn. Generations I-II [ edit | edit source ] Growl cannot affect a Pokémon behind a substitute . In the Generation I and II core series games, when an in-game opponent outside of the Battle Tower uses Growl, the move has a 25% chance to fail. Generations III-V [ edit | edit source ] Growl no longer has an additional chance to fail when used by an in-game opponent. Generation VI onwards [ edit | edit source ] Growl can now hit Pokémon even if they are behind a substitute. Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Legends: Z-A Category Status Accuracy 100% Standard Plus Move Power — Cooldown 6s Move wind-up time 30 frames Effect duration 12s 15s Additional effect — — — None

sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description Stad A technique that lowers the target's Attack power. Can normally be used up to 6 times. Stad2 A move that lowers the target's Attack power. Can normally be used up to six times. G S C Reduces the foe's Attack . R S E Colo XD Growls cutely to reduce the foe's Attack . FR LG The user growls in a cute way, making the foe lower its Attack . stat. D P Pt HG SS PBR The user growls in an endearing way, making the foe less wary. The target's Attack stat is lowered. B W B2 W2 The user growls in an endearing way, making the opposing team less wary. The foes' Attack stats are lowered. X Y OR AS The user growls in an endearing way, making opposing Pokémon less wary. This lowers their Attack stats. S M US UM P E The user growls in an endearing way, making opposing Pokémon less wary. This lowers their Attack stat. Sw Sh BD SP LA S V The user growls in an endearing way, making opposing Pokémon less wary. This lowers their Attack stats. ZA The user growls endearingly to make targets less wary, which lowers their Attack stats for a short time.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD R B Lowers the Attack of all foes in the room by one level. MD T D S Reduces the Attack of all enemies in the room by 1 level. B S L おなじへやの てきポケモンの こうげきを １だんかいさげる MD GtI S MD MD RT DX It lowers the Attack of enemies in the same room.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] Although the sound-based mechanic was not introduced until Generation III , Growl is listed in the move index number near other Generation I sound-based moves like Roar , Sing , and Supersonic .
